summary: Iteration 33 — 3 end-to-end user flows executed on mobile viewport 390x844. FLOW 1 (Company-Admin email monthly report) PASS. FLOW 2 (Employee Salary Policy round-trip + tier bonus on re-Process) PASS. FLOW 3 (Employee Profile Photo) PARTIAL FAIL — a real UI state-sync bug in /profile-photo prevents the preview + remove button from appearing after a page reload, even though /auth/me returns the photo. Cleanup complete; real super_admin doc untouched.

steps: Company-admin session token seeded via localStorage.setItem('llc_session_token', ...).
Home → tapped 'Process salary' ActionRow → landed on /payroll-run.
payroll-month-bar and payroll-process-btn render (default month = current: June 2026 in container clock — spec says previous full month, minor deviation).
Tapped payroll-process-btn → KPI row shows: 1 Employee, 0 h, ₹0 Gross total; email-report-bar rendered.
Expanded email-report-bar → tapped email-kind-combined → tapped email-recipients-self → tapped email-send-btn.

Result box shows: '0 sent, 1 failed' with '✗ admin_if33fab92d@test.local — http_403: {"statusCode":403,"name":"validation_error","messa…' → Resend HTTP call fired (403 from Resend is acceptable per spec: 'we just need the code path to fire').
flow_1_email_monthly_report.notes: Month bar defaulted to June 2026 (container clock ≈ July 2026), not 'previous full month' as the request suggested. Backend/UI logic works — send-report path fires end-to-end. Resend responded 403 validation_error because the throwaway admin email is on an unverified sending domain; spec explicitly allows 4xx from Resend.

steps: Company-admin navigated to /admin → policy-emp-<user_id> button visible for seeded employee.
Form contains policy-salary (1), policy-tier-1 (with 2 inputs: bonus + day), policy-save (1). All required testIDs render.
Filled: Monthly salary=30000, Tier 1 salary=2000, Tier 1 day=20.
Tapped policy-save → confirm dialog auto-accepted → router navigated back to /admin ✓.
Navigated to /payroll-run → tapped payroll-month-prev 7 times to reach November 2025.
Tapped payroll-process-btn → KPI shows: 1 Employees, 0 h, ₹28,400 Gross total.
Employee row card shows: ₹28,400 gross pill, 22d Present, 3d Absent, 5d Off, 0 h Hours, note 'Base salary: ₹30,000 → pro-rated 22/25 working days'.
flow_2_salary_policy_and_tier_bonus.notes: Row card does not print the literal string 'Tier 1 bonus' but shows the 'Base salary → pro-rated' breakdown note, which the spec accepts as an alternative.

steps_passed: Employee token seeded; navigated to Profile tab; profile-avatar visible (1).
Initial state (no photo): profile-photo-take=1, profile-photo-pick=1, profile-photo-remove=0 ✓.
POSTed tiny base64 PNG data URL to /api/me/profile-photo via browser fetch → 200 {"ok":true} ✓.
Verified /api/auth/me from the same browser context returns user.profile_photo_base64 populated (length=118) ✓.
steps_failed: After hard reload of /profile-photo (page.goto with cache-buster + 5s wait), profile-photo-preview count is still 0 and profile-photo-remove count is still 0.
The screen continues to render the initial-letter fallback avatar instead of the uploaded image.
flow_3_profile_photo.root_cause: Frontend state-sync bug in /app/frontend/app/profile-photo.tsx (L50-L52). The `preview` state is initialised with `useState<string | null>(user?.profile_photo_base64 || null)`. When the screen mounts BEFORE AuthContext has finished its /auth/me bootstrap (which is nearly always on a fresh page.goto), `user` is null and the initializer freezes `preview` at null. When AuthContext later populates `user` with `profile_photo_base64`, there is no `useEffect` to sync `preview` from the new user value, so the preview + remove-button branch of the JSX never renders.
flow_3_profile_photo.fix_suggestion: Add `useEffect(() => { setPreview(user?.profile_photo_base64 || null); }, [user?.profile_photo_base64]);` right after the useState declaration. This keeps `preview` in sync whenever the auth user is (re)hydrated.
flow_3_profile_photo.notes: Backend is correct — POST/DELETE /api/me/profile-photo work as expected (verified in iter_33 backend tests and re-verified via fetch here). The bug is purely UI state-management.

ui_bugs.issue: After the backend has a photo, revisiting /profile-photo does not render profile-photo-preview or profile-photo-remove because `preview` is initialised once from user?.profile_photo_base64 (which is null on first mount) and never re-synced when AuthContext populates user later.
ui_bugs.fix: Add useEffect to setPreview(user?.profile_photo_base64 || null) on user?.profile_photo_base64 change.

issues: Default month is the CURRENT month (June 2026 in the container clock), not the 'previous full month' as the task described. Confirm with product whether this is intended.

action_items: FIX (HIGH): Add a useEffect in /app/frontend/app/profile-photo.tsx to keep the local `preview` state synced with `user?.profile_photo_base64`. Otherwise revisiting the screen with an existing photo never displays it and users can't tap Remove.
REVIEW (LOW): Confirm whether /payroll-run should default to the previous full month or the current month. Task described 'previous full month' but the code defaults to the current month.
critical_code_review_comments: profile-photo.tsx L50-L52: useState initializer captures user?.profile_photo_base64 only once; when the AuthContext hydrates user asynchronously the local state does not update. This is a classic 'derived state not synced' bug — should be a useEffect or replaced with `user?.profile_photo_base64 ?? localPreview`.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 company (co_if33fab92d_*), 1 company_admin, 1 employee (join_date=2025-11-01, employee_code F...0001), 2 sessions, 22 IN attendance punches on Nov 2025 Mon-Sat dates. Cleanup verified: attendance=22 sessions=2 users=2 companies=1 deleted. Real super_admin (sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com) doc verified untouched post-run: role=super_admin, pin_must_change=true, pin_fail_count=2 (unchanged).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Repro for the profile-photo bug: (1) log in as any employee, (2) POST /api/me/profile-photo with a base64 PNG, (3) hard-navigate to /profile-photo — profile-photo-preview and profile-photo-remove never appear. /auth/me confirms the backend stored the field. Once the main agent adds the useEffect sync, retest by uploading via fetch and reloading; the preview + remove testIDs should appear immediately.
rca_of_issue: The <ProfilePhotoScreen> uses `useState<string | null>(user?.profile_photo_base64 || null)`. React's useState initializer runs only once. On a fresh navigation the AuthContext's /auth/me call is still in-flight, so `user` is null and preview freezes at null. When user later populates, no effect re-hydrates the local state. Fix: add `useEffect(() => setPreview(user?.profile_photo_base64 || null), [user?.profile_photo_base64])` after the useState declaration. This will cascade correctly with the existing upload/remove local-state flow because those handlers already call setPreview explicitly.
